reasoning: The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) stopped making new loans in March 2024, and all loans had a maximum term of 1 year. The FRED dataset confirms that the balance went to exactly zero by March 26, 2025, and has remained at 0.000 for nearly five months up through the August 17, 2025 cutoff. Since it is extremely rare for a fully closed and wound-down Federal Reserve 13(3) emergency facility to spontaneously reopen or exhibit sudden structural balance increases (and any future facility would likely get a new FRED series identifier), the probability of the balance increasing above zero is exceptionally low. Minor increases to probability are projected for more distant horizons only to account for extreme black-swan banking crises or strange accounting anomalies, keeping minimums around the specified 0.05 bound.
